summary: Backend-only iteration 4. Verified the emergentintegrations migration (EMERGENT_LLM_KEY / gpt-5.1 openai) on /api/ai/{health,chat,anomalies,sessions} and the new admin deletion endpoints (/api/admin/users/{id}/{soft-delete,dependencies,hard-delete} and same for /api/admin/plants/{id}) enforce Authorization bearer auth (401 without token, 401/403 on malformed token, never 500). Regressed core endpoints (/api/, /api/compliance/thresholds, /api/downtime/events, /api/alerts/feed, /api/blending/wells) still pass. Found and fixed one real regression in server.py where the section comment swallowed the /api/cron/compliance-evaluate decorator; that route was 404 in production. Full suite: 44/44 passing.

minor.issue: FIXED. Was 404 in prod: a section-header comment on server.py line 517 was on the same line as @api_router.post(...), commenting out the decorator. Split into two lines; endpoint now registered and responding 200/500.
Test only checks that Mongo _id is not leaked in list items — response shape (bare list vs {sessions:[...]}) was tolerated. Shape is stable but worth documenting.

action_items: No code action required from main agent for the review-request items — all 12 requested checks pass.

Optional: once a confirmed Supabase test user is available (see iteration_3 blocker), add positive-path admin tests (create user, soft-delete -> profile.status=='Suspended', hard-delete with dependency -> 409, then cleanup).
Optional: in admin_service._bearer_token, current error message 'Missing bearer token' is fine; if you want nicer UX, distinguish 'missing' vs 'wrong scheme' with 401 + WWW-Authenticate header.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 517 BUG (now FIXED): `# ---- Serverless-friendly cron endpoints ---------------------------------- @api_router.post(...)` on a single line commented the decorator; /api/cron/compliance-evaluate returned 404. Add a linter rule or a smoke test that asserts each @api_router decorator sits on its own line.

admin_service._service_client always creates a NEW Supabase client per request (no pool). For hot paths this is fine, but the docstring says 'service client' while it actually uses SUPABASE_ANON_KEY + postgrest.auth(jwt) — consider renaming to _user_scoped_client for clarity. Current behavior is correct (RLS-safe).

admin_service.hard_delete_user cannot remove the auth.users row (requires service role key). That limitation is documented in the response note, but the UI should warn the admin so the auth entry isn't orphaned.
ai_service.DEFAULT_MODEL = 'gpt-5.1' hardcoded; consider reading from env (AI_MODEL) so you can flip to gpt-5.2 without a code change.

test_pwri_backend::TestCronEndpoints::test_cron_compliance_evaluate_dev_mode asserts only `status_code in (200, 500)` which masked the 404 regression. Tighten to `== 200` (now that it works) or at least exclude 404/405.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (split stuck decorator line 517: section header vs @api_router.post('/cron/compliance-evaluate'))
/app/backend/tests/test_ai_and_admin.py (new: 21 tests covering AI migration + admin RBAC auth gating)
test_credentials: /app/memory/test_credentials.md is still empty. Admin endpoints tested only for auth-gating (401 paths) — no valid Supabase JWT available to exercise happy paths.
seed_data_creation: None new. TestBlendingWells cleans up its TEST_ entries; TestAlertsFeed leaves a dated TEST_alerts_plant blending audit row (<=7 days old, idempotent with earlier iterations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 12 requested checks pass: /api/ai/health returns ok=true gpt-5.1 openai; /api/ai/chat returns non-empty reply + session_id (emergentintegrations wired); /api/ai/anomalies returns {anomalies:[]} shape; all 6 admin endpoints return 401 without token and 401/403 (never 500) with malformed bearer; /api/ai/sessions list does not leak Mongo _id; 5 regression endpoints all green. New test file /app/backend/tests/test_ai_and_admin.py (21 tests). Bug fixed: /api/cron/compliance-evaluate was 404 because a section-header comment ate its @api_router.post decorator on server.py line 517 — now on its own line. Suite total 44/44 passing. If a valid Supabase JWT becomes available, extend test_ai_and_admin.py with TestAdminHappyPath to exercise soft-delete -> status flip and hard-delete -> 409 dependency-block.
rca of the issue: Root cause for the cron/compliance-evaluate 404: during the P2 cleanup pass (stray files removed), a code-block join left the section comment `# ---- Serverless-friendly cron endpoints ----------------------------------` on the same physical line as the decorator `@api_router.post("/cron/compliance-evaluate")`. Python treated everything after `#` as a comment, so the decorator never wrapped `cron_compliance_evaluate`, and FastAPI never registered the route. Repro: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w %{http_code} -X POST $URL/api/cron/compliance-evaluate` -> 404. Mitigation applied: split comment and decorator onto separate lines (search_replace in server.py). Prevention: tighten existing pytest assertion from `status_code in (200, 500)` to `== 200` so 404/405 regressions fail loudly; add a ruff/lint rule or sanity test iterating FastAPI routes and asserting expected set. Verified fix: /api/cron/compliance-evaluate now returns 200 and the full 44-test suite is green.
